en.extract:
The giant sunbird (Dreptes thomensis) is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is the only species in the genus Dreptes. It is endemic to the island of São Tomé (São Tomé and Príncipe), where it occurs in the central massif.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. It is threatened by habitat loss. The species was first described by José Vicente Barbosa du Bocage in 1889.


References


External links
BirdLife Species Factsheet.

The '''giant sunbird''' ('''''Dreptes thomensis''''') is a species of bird in the family [[Nectariniidae]]. It is the only species in the genus '''''Dreptes'''''.<ref>[http://datazone.birdlife.org/species/taxonomy HBW and BirdLife Taxonomic Checklist v3], accessed 16 January 2019</ref> It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the island of [[São Tomé Island|São Tomé]] ([[São Tomé and Príncipe]]), where it occurs in the central massif.<ref name="iucn status 17 November 2021" />

Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest|montane forests]]. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]]. The species was first described by [[José Vicente Barbosa du Bocage]] in 1889.
